label: Police impersonation scam
description: Caller claims to be police/Bukit Aman/MACC asking the victim to transfer money to a 'verification account'.
advice: Police never ask you to transfer money to verify your identity. Hang up and call 999.

label: Advance-fee loan scam
description: Promises a quick loan but requires a 'processing fee' upfront.
advice: Licensed lenders never ask for upfront fees by eWallet.

label: Parcel/courier scam
description: Caller pretends to be from a courier saying a parcel is held due to illegal contents.
advice: Couriers don't ask for payment via personal transfer. Verify on the official courier app.

label: Love / investment scam
description: Online romantic interest pressures victim to invest in a 'guaranteed return' platform.
advice: Anyone you've never met in person who asks for money is almost always a scammer.

label: Child impulse / in-game purchase
description: Large in-app gaming or merchandise purchase initiated by a young user, often above the agreed weekly limit.
advice: Talk to your child about the limit. Consider approving a smaller, age-appropriate amount.
